name: 3rd Shot Pickleball Reno
description: Come play pickleball at 3rd Shot Pickleball Reno in Reno, NV! There are 9 indoor acrylic courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The facility includes amenities like food, water, restrooms, lights, wheelchair accessibility, and trainers or lessons. A one-time fee is required to play. The courts can be reserved. Guests and Members welcome!
open_play_schedule:
6am - 10pm
See Court Reserve app to sign up and find sessions.
fees: Fee required — Open to the public, with Guest Play and Membership options available.

name: Alf Sorensen Community Center
description: Alf Sorensen Community Center is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Sparks, NV. There are 2 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The facility includes amenities like restrooms and water. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: Incline Village Recreation Center Gym
description:
These courts are NOT available in late spring-summer-early fall.
They are only available when outdoor play is not possible because of weather.
Never in summer.

name: Incline Village Tennis & Pickelball Center
description:
Beautiful courts Located in Incline Village, NV in a forest with a stream running by.  
Free 18 hole disc golf course all around the courts.
Short walk to the beach of Lake Tahoe.
Open play every day.
Season pass, daily fee or punch cards all available.
open_play_schedule:
Open every day from dawn to dusk
Open play every morning until 12p/1p or later
fees: Fee required — Season passes, daily fee or punch cards available.

name: Pah Rah Mountain Park
description: Come play pickleball at Pah Rah Mountain Park in Sparks, NV! There are 2 outdoor concrete and hard courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The courts are free. The facility includes amenities like restrooms and water.

name: Plumas Alpine/Reno Tennis Center
description: Plumas Alpine/Reno Tennis Center is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Reno, NV. There are 24 outdoor asphalt and concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. A one-time fee is required to play. You can reserve the courts. Amenities include restrooms, water, lights, trainers or lessons, wheelchair accessibility, a pro shop or equipment store, and youth programming.

name: Summit Christian Church
description: Come visit us and play on our 3 brand new courts. All available for open play. With court seating, an onsite cafe, water bottle fill stations and restrooms we have everything you need to enjoy a day of friendly pickleball!
